reasoning: The 360-day window from March 2025 to February 2026 has an average of ~146.6 battles per 30-day period. While the baseline probability of any 30-day period exceeding the 360-day average is typically around 40-45% due to the bursty right-skewed nature of conflict data (where massive spikes pull the mean up above the median), current trends in the DRC point to an actively escalating conflict *right now*. An ongoing major offensive by M23 and other groups that captured Uvira in December 2025 has transitioned into further 'escalated conflict and intensified M23 attacks' through January and February 2026, according to recent UN OCHA and ACLED reports. Because the highest intensity of the conflict appears to be occurring at the very end of the 360-day window (and extending into the first week of March), the focal 30-day period (Feb 6 - Mar 7, 2026) is highly likely to feature an above-average number of battle events compared to the year-long baseline, which includes presumably quieter periods in mid-2025.
